name: Olena Koval
description: Olena keeps guest communication clear from the first reservation request through the final service details.
tips: Her focus is calm coordination, thoughtful table planning, and making private visits feel well prepared.
body: Olena works across reservations, guest follow-up, and event coordination so every visit has a clear point of contact before service begins.
She helps the team balance practical floor planning with personal hospitality, especially for celebrations, group dinners, and guests with timing or dietary notes.
Her profile reflects the business standard for warm communication that stays precise, responsive, and useful.
highlights: Supports private dining and celebration planning

name: Andrii Melnyk
description: Andrii guides the dining room service flow, helps guests choose dishes, and keeps table service smooth during busy hours.
tips: Ask Andrii for dish timing, table setup, or simple menu pairing advice when planning a relaxed dinner.
body: Andrii is the kind of waiter guests remember because he keeps service attentive without making it feel heavy.
He supports the team during breakfast, lunch, and dinner service, helps with table coordination, and makes sure guest requests reach the kitchen or bar clearly.
His public profile is useful for restaurants that want to show that service is not only about taking orders, but also about care, timing, and calm communication.
highlights: Helps guests choose from the menu

name: Maksym Petrenko
description: Maksym prepares seasonal dishes and can personally explain ingredients, cooking ideas, and menu specials to guests.
tips: Ask Maksym about seasonal dishes, local products, or what to try if you want something simple but memorable.
body: Maksym represents the kitchen in a public-facing way, especially when guests are curious about the story behind a dish.
He can support tasting evenings, private dinners, seasonal menu presentations, and short table conversations when the service format allows it.
This profile helps the business show that food has a real team behind it, not just a menu card.
highlights: Supports tasting and private dinner formats
Connects local ingredients with simple presentation

name: Sofia Hnatiuk
description: Sofia prepares coffee, tea, and seasonal drinks while helping guests find the right drink for their mood.
tips: Ask Sofia what coffee works best with dessert or which seasonal drink is worth trying today.
body: Sofia brings the cafe part of the business to life through coffee, small conversations, and drink recommendations.
Her role is public because many guests interact directly with the barista during quick visits, takeaway orders, or afternoon coffee breaks.
A barista profile works well for horeca templates because coffee is often a reason guests return even when they are not planning a full meal.
highlights: Prepares espresso, latte, cappuccino, and tea

name: Roman Shevchenko
description: Roman prepares classic and signature drinks, supports evening service, and helps guests choose cocktails, wine, or soft drinks.
tips: Ask Roman for a drink recommendation based on your dinner, celebration format, or preferred flavor.
body: Roman is a public-facing bar team member because guests often want direct recommendations for cocktails, wine, beer, or non-alcoholic drinks.
He helps the bar stay organized during dinner service, private events, and slower evenings when guests want a more personal drink experience.
His profile can be used to make the bar feel more human and to highlight signature drinks without turning the page into a full bar menu.
highlights: Recommends drinks for dinner or events

name: Iryna Moroz
description: Iryna welcomes hotel guests, supports check-in and check-out, and helps visitors understand room, breakfast, and service details.
tips: Ask Iryna about check-in time, breakfast, parking, family rooms, or available guest services.
body: Iryna is the first point of contact for many hotel guests, so her role is naturally public-facing.
She helps guests understand practical details before and during their stay, including room readiness, breakfast options, parking, and extra services.
A receptionist profile is useful for hotel templates because it gives guests confidence that there is a real person supporting their stay.
highlights: Helps with room and booking questions
Coordinates guest requests with hotel teams

name: Daria Lysenko
description: Daria helps guests plan birthdays, banquets, group dinners, weddings, and business meetings with clear timing and service details.
tips: Ask Daria about guest count, menu options, seating, timing, decorations, and event flow.
body: Daria turns event inquiries into clear plans that guests can understand before they confirm a date.
She coordinates with the kitchen, service team, bar, and management to keep birthdays, banquets, weddings, and group dinners organized.
Her public profile is useful because event guests often need a trusted person who can explain the process simply.
highlights: Supports guests from inquiry to event day

name: Nazar Boyko
description: Nazar welcomes guests in the spa area, explains basic rules, coordinates time slots, and keeps the relax zone calm and comfortable.
tips: Ask Nazar about available spa time, sauna rules, towels, rest areas, and guest capacity.
body: Nazar is a public wellness team member because spa guests often need simple guidance before using sauna, hammam, pool, or rest areas.
He supports a calm guest flow, helps with towels and basic comfort, and keeps guests informed about timing and rules.
A spa host profile helps the business make wellness services feel more approachable and organized.
highlights: Welcomes guests in the spa zone
Explains sauna and relax area rules
Coordinates spa time slots and comfort

name: Kateryna Rudenko
description: Kateryna leads room preparation standards and helps keep hotel spaces clean, calm, and ready for guest arrival.
tips: Her work is mostly behind the scenes, but guests feel it through clean rooms, fresh linen, and prepared spaces.
body: Kateryna is not usually part of long guest conversations, but her role is public enough to show because room comfort starts with housekeeping.
She helps maintain preparation standards for rooms, shared corridors, linen, and guest-ready spaces.
A housekeeping lead profile shows respect for the team members who make hotel stays feel clean, calm, and reliable.
highlights: Supports clean and comfortable guest spaces
